skill.name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter-java
skill.description: Guide Java developers through Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry exporter setup, tracing, metrics, exception recording, and migration to the recommended autoconfigure package.
skill.summary: Configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry telemetry for Java and plan migration from the deprecated exporter.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, credential access, reconnaissance, and a dangerous combination heuristic. Review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, Java API names, Azure documentation URLs, and placeholder connection strings. The real risks are low and expected for an observability skill: telemetry export to Azure Monitor and environment-variable handling.

medium_findings.title: Deprecated Package Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill documents a deprecated Java exporter and recommends migration to azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure. This is not malicious, but users could copy deprecated dependency guidance if they miss the migration notice.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The deprecation notice and migration section are explicit. The risk is operational compatibility risk, not malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Telemetry Export Requires Secret Handling
low_findings.description: The skill shows Azure Monitor connection-string configuration through an environment variable and a placeholder explicit connection string. This is expected for telemetry export, but users must avoid hardcoding real instrumentation keys in source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines directly document connection-string usage. The examples use placeholders, so the issue is safe usage guidance rather than confirmed secret exposure.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command execution, weak cryptography, credential directory, reconnaissance, and dangerous-combination findings were reviewed as false positives. Evidence points to Markdown fences, Java snippets, Azure URLs, metric names, and documentation links rather than executable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is documentation only and contains no scripts or executable hooks. The suspicious patterns are explained by code examples and reference links.

content.value_statement: Java teams need reliable traces, metrics, and exception data in Azure Monitor. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for OpenTelemetry setup and exporter migration.
actual_capabilities: Explains Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry exporter installation for Java projects.
Provides examples for spans, attributes, nested spans, and exception recording.
Describes basic OpenTelemetry metrics with counters and histograms.
Links to official Azure, Maven, and OpenTelemetry reference resources.
limitations: The documented exporter package is deprecated and should not be the default for new projects.
The skill does not run Java code or validate live Azure Monitor ingestion.
Version values use examples and require confirmation against current Azure SDK releases.
It does not cover every OpenTelemetry semantic convention or instrumentation library.

use_cases.title: Plan a Migration
use_cases.description: Replace the deprecated exporter dependency with the Azure Monitor autoconfigure package.

use_cases.title: Add Service Tracing
use_cases.description: Create spans, attributes, nested operations, and exception recording in a Java service.

use_cases.title: Review Observability Patterns
use_cases.description: Check whether Java telemetry examples follow practical OpenTelemetry and Azure Monitor practices.

prompt_templates.title: Start Java Telemetry
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a Java service
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for a Java service. Explain the recommended package and environment variable setup.

prompt_templates.title: Add Trace Spans
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add OpenTelemetry spans, attributes, and exception recording for a Java request handler that sends telemetry to Azure Monitor.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate Deprecated Exporter
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Java Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry setup and outline the migration from azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter to autoconfigure.

prompt_templates.title: Design Observability Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an observability plan for a Java service using Azure Monitor, OpenTelemetry spans, metrics, exception recording, and semantic attributes.

output_examples.input: I need to send Java service traces to Azure Monitor.
output: Recommendation to use the autoconfigure package.
Connection-string setup through an environment variable.
A short tracing flow with span creation and cleanup.

output_examples.input: I still use the deprecated Azure Monitor exporter dependency.
output: Migration notes for replacing the dependency.
References to the Azure migration guide.

output_examples.input: I want better diagnostics for failed Java operations.
output: Guidance for recording exceptions on spans.
Metrics ideas for request counts and latency.

best_practices: Use the autoconfigure package for new Java Azure Monitor instrumentation.
Store real Application Insights connection strings outside source control.
Name spans clearly and include only useful, non-sensitive attributes.
anti_patterns: Do not start new projects on the deprecated exporter package.
Do not hardcode production connection strings in Java source files.
Do not add high-cardinality or sensitive values as span attributes.

faq.question: Is this skill for new Java projects?
faq.answer: It can help, but new projects should prefer azure-monitor-opentelemetry-autoconfigure.

faq.question: Does it send telemetry by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides setup guidance and examples for Java applications.

faq.question: Can it help with Application Insights?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples target Azure Monitor and Application Insights connection-string configuration.

faq.question: Does it include metrics guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes basic OpenTelemetry counter and histogram examples.

faq.question: Can I paste production connection strings into prompts?
faq.answer: No. Use placeholders and keep production secrets out of prompts and source files.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
